title: Recognizing the importance of sleep health and expressing support for the designation of the week of March 9 through March 15, 2025, as "Sleep Awareness Week".
plain_summary: This bill wants Congress to officially recognize the week of March 9-15, 2025 as Sleep Awareness Week. The bill says that getting enough sleep is very important for staying healthy, but many Americans do not sleep enough. When people do not get enough sleep, it can cause serious health problems. The bill encourages people to make sleep a priority and talk to their doctors about any sleep problems they might have.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution recognizes how important sleep health is and supports naming the week of March ninth through March fifteenth, twenty twenty-five, as Sleep Awareness Week.

sections.title: Findings About Sleep Health
sections.text: Sleep is essential for good health, well-being, and safety. Promoting science-based sleep health practices will improve public health overall. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention says most adults need at least seven hours of sleep each night. However, the National Sleep Foundation found in their twenty twenty-five poll that sixty percent of Americans do not regularly get the recommended amount of sleep each night. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention reports that thirty-five percent of all adults in the United States sleep less than seven hours per night on average. Not getting enough sleep can cause poor memory, high stress levels, problems with how the body processes food, and higher risk of injuries and accidents. Over time, not sleeping enough can increase the risk of diabetes, stroke, heart disease, high blood pressure, depression, and other health problems. Not being able to sleep can be a sign of depression and anxiety, and sleep problems can also make mental health conditions worse or help cause them. Many studies have found that Black Americans are less likely than White Americans to get enough sleep, and Black Americans may also get lower quality sleep. Congress created the National Center on Sleep Disorders Research in nineteen ninety-three to help coordinate sleep and body clock research within the National Institutes of Health and other federal agencies. For over twenty years, the National Sleep Foundation has organized Sleep Awareness Week to promote the benefits of healthy sleep and show how important it is for overall health and well-being. Many states and organizations across the country recognize and participate in Sleep Awareness Week.

sections.title: House of Representatives Resolution
sections.text: The House of Representatives recognizes that sleep health is important for increasing health and well-being. It supports naming a Sleep Awareness Week and supports the goals and efforts of Sleep Awareness Week. The House encourages public health officials, healthcare providers, teachers, parents, and others to do their part to promote getting enough sleep. It encourages the people of the United States to make their sleep health a priority, practice good sleep habits, and talk about their sleep with their healthcare provider. The House also recognizes that policies to improve sleep health continue to be important.
